mcqs.question: What is the primary reason Apache Spark is 10-100x faster than Hadoop MapReduce?

options.text: Spark uses more powerful hardware
Spark performs in-memory computations instead of disk-based processing
Spark has fewer fault tolerance mechanisms
Spark only processes small datasets

mcqs.explanation: Spark achieves superior performance by caching data in memory across operations, avoiding the repeated disk I/O that MapReduce requires between Map and Reduce stages.

mcqs.question: In Spark, what is an RDD (Resilient Distributed Dataset)?

options.text: A mutable collection stored on a single node
An immutable, distributed collection that can be processed in parallel with fault tolerance
A database table with ACID properties
A streaming data source

mcqs.explanation: RDD is Spark's fundamental data structure - an immutable, distributed collection partitioned across the cluster. It's resilient through lineage-based fault tolerance.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between Transformations and Actions in Spark?

options.text: Transformations modify RDDs in place, Actions create new RDDs
Transformations are eager, Actions are lazy
Transformations are lazy operations that create new RDDs, Actions are eager operations that trigger computation
No difference, both execute immediately

mcqs.explanation: Transformations (map, filter) are lazy - they build a computation DAG. Actions (count, collect) are eager - they trigger execution of the entire DAG and return results.

mcqs.question: Which Spark component provides scalable machine learning algorithms?

options.text: Spark SQL
Spark Streaming
MLlib
GraphX

mcqs.explanation: MLlib is Spark's machine learning library providing scalable algorithms for classification, regression, clustering, and collaborative filtering, ideal for IoT predictive analytics.

mcqs.question: How does Spark Streaming process real-time data?

options.text: True event-by-event processing like Apache Storm
Divides data into micro-batches processed as RDDs at regular intervals
Stores all data first, then processes in one large batch
Uses MapReduce internally for stream processing

mcqs.explanation: Spark Streaming uses micro-batch processing: live data is divided into small time intervals (e.g., 1-5 seconds), each processed as an RDD batch. This provides near real-time processing while reusing Spark's batch engine.

mcqs.question: How does Spark achieve fault tolerance for RDDs?

options.text: By replicating all data across 3 nodes like HDFS
By recording transformation lineage and recomputing lost partitions from parent RDDs
By writing all intermediate results to disk
By checkpointing every RDD automatically

mcqs.explanation: Spark uses lineage-based fault tolerance: each RDD records its transformation history. If a partition is lost, Spark recomputes it from parent RDDs using the lineage graph, without data replication overhead.

mcqs.question: An IoT platform processes sensor data two ways: (1) Historical batch analysis of 1TB daily data, (2) Real-time anomaly detection with <1 second latency. Which Spark approach is most suitable?

options.text: Only MapReduce for both batch and streaming
Spark SQL for batch analysis and Spark Streaming with micro-batches for near real-time anomaly detection
Separate systems: Hadoop for batch, Storm for streaming
Only Spark Streaming for all processing

mcqs.explanation: Spark provides a unified platform: Spark SQL handles 1TB batch processing with in-memory caching, while Spark Streaming processes micro-batches for near real-time detection. This unified approach simplifies architecture and enables code reuse.

mcqs.question: A data scientist wants to train a machine learning model on 6 months of IoT sensor data (500GB) and then apply it to real-time sensor streams. Which Spark workflow is optimal?

options.text: Train with MLlib on batch data, serialize model, load in Spark Streaming pipeline for real-time scoring
Use only Spark Streaming for both training and prediction
Export data to a separate ML platform, import predictions back
Use Spark SQL for both model training and real-time prediction

mcqs.explanation: The optimal workflow: MLlib trains on historical batch data (500GB), the trained model is serialized and loaded into a Spark Streaming pipeline that scores each micro-batch in real-time. This leverages Spark's unified platform for the complete ML lifecycle.
